# Argus

> Argus is a standalone search broker that routes queries across multiple web-search providers (SearXNG, Brave, Serper, Tavily, Exa) with automatic fallback, Reciprocal Rank Fusion ranking, health tracking, and budget enforcement. Connect via HTTP API, CLI, MCP server, or Python import.

## Installation

> pip install -e ".[mcp]" to install with MCP support. Configure provider API keys in .env. Run `argus serve` to start the HTTP API on port 8000, or `argus mcp serve` to start the MCP server over stdio.

## MCP Server

> Start with `argus mcp serve`. Tools: search_web, recover_url, expand_links, search_health, search_budgets, test_provider. Add to Claude Code, Cursor, or VS Code by adding the MCP config JSON to your client settings. See README for copy-paste configs.

## HTTP API

> POST /api/search with {"query": "...", "mode": "discovery", "max_results": 10}. OpenAPI docs at /docs. Endpoints: /api/search, /api/recover-url, /api/expand, /api/health, /api/health/detail, /api/budgets, /api/test-provider.

## Search Modes

> discovery: find related pages and canonical sources. recovery: find a moved or dead URL. grounding: few live sources for fact-checking. research: broad exploratory retrieval.

## Python SDK

> from argus.broker.router import create_broker; from argus.models import SearchQuery, SearchMode; broker = create_broker(); response = await broker.search(SearchQuery(query="...", mode=SearchMode.DISCOVERY))

## CLI

> argus search -q "query" --mode discovery; argus recover-url -u "https://dead.link"; argus health; argus budgets; argus test-provider -p brave

## Documentation

> README.md — full documentation with quick start, integration examples, and configuration reference
> docs/providers.md — provider setup, Docker networking, and configuration
> CLAUDE.md — developer guide and architecture overview
> CONTRIBUTING.md — contribution guide and adding providers
